title: Finale: state monotonicity of badness under vertex deletion and verify Cor 2.7 hypotheses on T_i

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The Finale applies Corollary 2.7 to each subtree T_i at the neighbor vertex v_i. This requires:
(a) T_i has NO bad vertex. Justify via monotonicity: deleting v only decreases degrees, so a non-bad vertex of T stays non-bad in T_i; the unique bad vertex v is removed. Add this one-line lemma.
(b) Strict inequality w(v_i) > d_{T_i}(v_i). Since d_{T_i}(v_i) = d_T(v_i) - 1 and v_i is not bad (w(v_i) >= d_T(v_i)), we get w(v_i) >= d_T(v_i) = d_{T_i}(v_i)+1 > d_{T_i}(v_i).
These checks are short but are the hypotheses that license Cor 2.7 (=> lambda_i irreducible), without which the dichotomy's case (i) is not killed.
